RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 115-J, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Mike Costlow, a Republican, proudly represents part of St. Charles County (District 108) in the Missouri House of Representatives. He was elected to his first two-year term in November 2024.
A Jacksonville, FL native, Rep. Costlow graduated from Lowndes County High School in 2005 and earned an MBA from Western Governors University in 2018.
Before joining the House, Rep. Costlow served as an alderman and board president for Dardenne Prairie, where he championed fiscal responsibility and passed safety measures to protect residents. Rep. Costlow intends to carry similar issues in the legislature, focusing on supporting economic growth, removing politics from classrooms, expanding school choices for families, and defending Missourians against federal overreach. He is dedicated to serving with integrity to ensure a bright future for Missouri.
Rep. Costlow is a family man, married to his wife, Jaycee, and enjoys spending his free time with her and their five children: Michael, Emily, Hanna, Elias, and Henry.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Establishes the "Missouri Task Force on Nonprofit Safety and Security"
Modifies provisions relating to firearm suppressors
Requires every school district to comply with enhanced safety and security standards
Modifies provisions for underground facilities notification requirements
Modifies provisions relating to certain convention and sports facility authorities
Creates provisions relating to an option to opt out of coverage under the Missouri consolidated health care plan and receive an annual stipend instead
Adds provisions relating to the offense of stealing
Modifies provisions relating to unlawful discriminatory practices
Modifies provisions relating to the sexual offender registry
Modifies provisions relating to transportation network companies and establishes the offense of impersonating a transportation network company driver
Authorizes a transient guest tax for the promotion of tourism in any municipality, upon voter approval
Proposes a constitutional amendment granting homestead and personal property tax exemptions to certain veterans proportional to the veteran's disability rating
Proposes a constitutional amendment to assert the right of parents to participate in and direct the education of their children free from government interference

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies when a registration-inspection certificate can be granted based on the grantees handling of certain nonnative invasive plant species
Grants the air conservation commission the authority to create standards and guidelines
Establishes provisions relating to civil liability for publishing or distributing material harmful to minors on the internet
Modifies provisions relating to public safety
Creates provisions relating to health care provider participation in health insurance plans
Requires the general assembly to approve proposed administrative rules
Modifies provisions relating to the list of diseases required to be reported to the department of health and senior services
Bans pesticides and pesticide ingredients that were made in China
Designates the last week in April each year as "Infertility Awareness Week"
